rules.name: Required Sections Present
rules.query:
Confirm presence of the standard financial-statement sections: Cover Page, Table of Contents, Balance Sheet (or Statement of Assets and Liabilities), Statement of Operations, Statement of Changes in Partners'/Members' Capital, Statement of Cash Flows, and Schedule of Investments. Notes to Financial Statements must be present only if applicable. Identify sections by their page headings or by labels in the Table of Contents.

Pass criteria: All required sections are present in the document.

Fail criteria: Any required section is missing.

needs_review when a section's identity is ambiguous from headings alone.
rules.description: Confirms the document contains every required financial-statement section (Cover, TOC, BS, OPS, CHG, SCF, Notes; SOI when applicable).

rules.name: TOC Page Numbers Match Actual Pages
rules.query:
For each entry in the Table of Contents, verify that the page number listed in the TOC matches the actual page on which that section starts. Use the printed page number on each section's first page. Normalize: trim whitespace, ignore leading dot leaders, case-insensitive match on section labels.

Pass criteria: Every TOC entry's stated page number equals the actual starting page of that section.

Fail criteria: Any TOC entry points to the wrong page or the page is missing.

needs_review when a TOC entry refers to a section that is explicitly stated as being present in an attached external document.
rules.description: Verifies every Table of Contents page reference matches the actual starting page of that section in the document.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Every TOC page reference matches the actual section start page.

rules.name: TOC Completeness (no orphan sections)
rules.query:
Perform a two-way check between the Table of Contents and the section headings throughout the document:
  (1) every section heading found in the document appears as a TOC entry, and
  (2) every TOC entry corresponds to a real section in the document.

Match labels case-insensitively, ignoring trailing punctuation and dot leaders.

Pass criteria: Every section in the document is represented in the TOC and every TOC entry maps to a real section.

Fail criteria: A section appears in the document with no TOC coverage, or a TOC entry has no matching section.

needs_review when the mismatch is related to a TOC entry referring to a section that is explicitly stated as being present in an attached external document.
rules.description: Two-way check that every section is listed in the TOC and every TOC entry points to a real section (no orphans on either side).
rules.acceptance_criteria: All sections listed in TOC; all TOC entries map to real sections.

rules.name: Period-End Date Consistency Across Pack
rules.query:
Extract every period-end reference across all pages. These typically appear after phrases such as 'Period End', 'As of', 'For the Period Ended', 'For the Year Ended', and as standalone period-end captions on each statement and on the cover. Normalize each into a canonical calendar date: accept formats 'MMMM D, YYYY', 'YYYY-MM-DD', and 'M/D/YYYY'. Strip trailing punctuation; compare case-insensitively. If a reference includes a weekday, validate that the weekday is correct for the stated date.

Pass criteria: Every period-end reference across the document resolves to the same calendar date, and any weekday/date pair is internally valid.

Fail criteria: Any reference resolves to a different calendar date, or a weekday/date pair is invalid.

needs_review when a reference is ambiguous (e.g., 'recent quarter') and cannot be normalized to a specific date.
rules.description: All period-end references across the full pack must resolve to the same calendar date; phrasing/case/punctuation differences are ignored.
rules.acceptance_criteria: All period-end references resolve to the same calendar date.

rules.name: Repeated Totals Without Basis
rules.description: Detects totals or line captions that repeat across statements without a legitimate tie-out basis (suspected copy/paste errors).
rules.acceptance_criteria: No unjustified duplicate totals or captions across statements.

rules.name: Cash Tie-Out (BS vs SCF)
rules.query:
You have the Balance Sheet and the Statement of Cash Flows in context. Capture two amounts and compare them:
  (1) the 'Cash and cash equivalents' line on the Balance Sheet (final labeled amount, not a sub-component);
  (2) the 'Cash and cash equivalents at end of period' line on the Statement of Cash Flows (accept label variants such as 'Cash at end of period' or 'Cash and cash equivalents, end of period').

Normalize number formats: remove '$', commas; treat parentheses as negative. Verify both statements use the same scale (both 'in thousands' or both 'in millions').

Pass criteria: BS ending cash equals SCF ending cash, tolerance 0.

Fail criteria: Mismatch >= $1 (in the stated scale).

needs_review when one of the two labels cannot be located unambiguously, or when the two statements use different scales.
rules.description: Ending cash on the Balance Sheet must equal ending cash on the Statement of Cash Flows.

rules.name: Partners' Capital Tie-Out (BS vs CHG)
sections: Statement of Changes in Partners' Capital
rules.query:
You have the Balance Sheet and the Statement of Changes in Partners'/Members' Capital in context. Capture two amounts and compare them:
  (1) Partners' Capital (or Members' Capital, or 'Net assets' when that is the labeled equity total on the Balance Sheet -- typical for feeder funds);
  (2) the ending balance on the Statement of Changes, identified by labels such as 'Partners' capital at end of period', 'Members' capital at end of period', or 'Balance at end of period'.

Use the final labeled total only; ignore intermediate subtotals. Normalize number formats: remove '$', commas; treat parentheses as negative.

Pass criteria: BS Partners'/Members' Capital equals CHG ending total, tolerance 0.

Fail criteria: Mismatch >= $1.

needs_review when the BS does not present a standalone capital line and the 'Net assets' fallback assumption is uncertain.
rules.description: Partners'/Members' Capital on the Balance Sheet must equal the ending total on the Statement of Changes in Partners' Capital.

rules.name: Net Gain/Loss Three-Way Tie-Out (OPS vs CHG vs SCF)
sections: Statement of Changes in Partners' Capital
rules.query:
You have the Statement of Operations, the Statement of Changes in Partners' Capital, and the Statement of Cash Flows in context. Capture the period's bottom-line performance figure from each statement and three-way compare.

Typical labels:
  - OPS: 'Net increase (decrease) in partners' capital resulting from operations' (or 'Net income', 'Net investment gain/(loss)');
  - CHG: the same 'Net increase ... resulting from operations' line in the activity rollforward;
  - SCF: the same line at the top of the operating-activities reconciliation.

If surface labels differ across the three statements (e.g., one says 'Net income' and another says 'Net increase in partners' capital resulting from operations and potential incentive allocation'), use the line that represents the same economic figure. Normalize number formats: remove '$', commas; treat parentheses as negative.

Pass criteria: The same value appears on all three statements, tolerance 0.

Fail criteria: Any pairwise mismatch >= $1.

needs_review when the corresponding line cannot be unambiguously located on one of the three statements.
rules.description: Net increase/(decrease) in partners' capital from operations must match across the Statement of Operations, Statement of Changes, and Statement of Cash Flows.

rules.name: Investments Tie-Out (SOI vs BS)
rules.query:
You have the Schedule of Investments and the Balance Sheet in context. Capture two amounts and compare them:
  (1) the SOI total fair value, identified by labels such as 'Total investments at fair value' or 'Total investments' on the bottom-row totals of the SOI;
  (2) the BS investments line, identified by labels such as 'Investments, at fair value' or 'Investments' (use the line that reflects investments measured at fair value, not amortized cost).

Normalize number formats: remove '$', commas; treat parentheses as negative. Ensure both statements are read in the same scale.

Pass criteria: SOI total fair value equals BS investments line, tolerance 0.

Fail criteria: Mismatch >= $1.

needs_review when the BS investments line presentation is ambiguous (e.g., separate fair value and cost columns) or when SOI subtotaling style makes the grand total uncertain.
rules.description: Total fair value of investments on the Schedule of Investments must equal the 'Investments, at fair value' line on the Balance Sheet.

rules.name: Balance Sheet Tie-Outs (Rollup)
sections: Statement of Changes in Partners' Capital
rules.query:
You have the Balance Sheet, the Statement of Cash Flows, the Statement of Changes in Partners'/Members' Capital, and the Schedule of Investments in context. This is a rollup of the cross-statement tie-outs that originate on the Balance Sheet -- run all three and aggregate the verdict:
  (a) BS ending cash = SCF ending cash;
  (b) BS Partners'/Members' Capital (or 'Net assets' when that is the BS equity line) = CHG ending total;
  (c) BS investments tie to the SOI on BOTH bases:
        (c1) BS 'Investments, at fair value' (or equivalent FMV line) = SOI total fair value;
        (c2) BS 'Investments, at cost' (or equivalent cost line) = SOI total cost.

For (c2), only evaluate the cost tie when the Balance Sheet discloses an investments-at-cost line (commonly shown alongside fair value, or parenthetically as 'cost $X'); if no cost figure is disclosed on the BS, treat (c2) as not_applicable rather than fail.

Normalize number formats: remove '$', commas; treat parentheses as negative. Ensure all statements are read in the same scale (all in thousands, or all in millions).

Pass criteria: All applicable tie-outs match exactly (tolerance 0) -- (a), (b), and (c1) always; (c2) when a BS cost line is disclosed.

Fail criteria: Any applicable tie-out has a mismatch >= $1.

needs_review when a tie-out is partially ambiguous (label match uncertain), when the BS cost disclosure format is unclear, or when SOI subtotaling style makes a grand total uncertain.
rules.description: Rollup of Balance Sheet cross-statement ties: cash to SCF, Partners'/Members' Capital to CHG, and investments to SOI on both fair value and cost bases.
rules.acceptance_criteria: All applicable BS tie-outs (cash, capital, investments FMV, and investments cost when disclosed) match exactly.

rules.name: SOI Percentage of Members'/Partners' Capital Tie
rules.query:
You have the Schedule of Investments and the Balance Sheet in context. If the SOI does not disclose a percentage-of-capital column, return not_applicable.

Capture the Members'/Partners' Capital total from the Balance Sheet (labels: 'Members' capital', 'Partners' capital', or 'Net assets' when that is the labeled equity total). For each investment row on the SOI that discloses a percentage of Members'/Partners' Capital, recompute the percentage as (investment fair value / total Members'/Partners' Capital) * 100, rounded to 2 decimal places. Compare the recomputed value to the disclosed percentage on the same SOI line. Apply a 0.02 percentage-point tolerance to absorb half-cent rounding (e.g., 97.56% vs 97.57% should pass). Normalize number formats: remove '$', commas, '%' signs; treat parentheses as negative.

Pass criteria: Every SOI line's disclosed percentage matches the recomputed percentage within +/- 0.02 percentage points.

Fail criteria: Any SOI line differs from the recomputed value by more than 0.02 percentage points.

needs_review when an SOI row's fair value or percentage cell is unreadable.
rules.description: Each SOI line's disclosed percentage of Members'/Partners' Capital must equal (fair value / capital total) * 100, rounded to 2 decimals, within a 0.02 pp tolerance.
